message:
test(atc + mailbox-verdict): rollup restore round-trip coverage + tracing subscriber probe for drop_close events

Two test-only additions.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs
- Extend `atc_rollup_snapshot_payload_preserves_compacted_baseline_fields`
  with a second phase: after asserting the snapshot payload preserves
  the compacted_* fields, mutate the row in-place (wrong subsystem,
  wrong effect_kind, wrong risk_tier, zeroed compacted_*), then
  `restore_atc_rollups(..., snapshot.payload, ...)` and assert the
  restored row exactly matches the original snapshot — including
  subsystem/effect_kind/risk_tier (which are now part of the UPSERT
  column list) and every compacted_* counter including
  `compacted_last_updated_ts`.
- Pins the "snapshot -> restore is a faithful round-trip, including
  semantic dimensions, not just counters" contract that the last two
  snapshot/restore commits set up.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/mailbox_verdict.rs
- Add `EventCapture` / `CapturedEvent` / `EventFieldCapture` tracing
  subscriber harness inside `#[cfg(test)]`.
- `EventCapture::saw_drop_close()` returns true iff a tracing event
  with target `fsqlite::runtime` emitted a field named `event` whose
  debug representation contains `"drop_close"` — this is the
  FrankenConnection "dropped without explicit close()" warn we went
  out of our way to suppress on the server side via
  `DbConnGuard::close_sync` wrapping. The subscriber is designed so a
  future regression in that wrapping is caught immediately by any
  test that pipes database work through the verdict path.
- Uses an `AtomicU64` for span-id allocation so the subscriber is
  Send + Sync and can be installed concurrently with other tests in
  the same process.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
